commit.message:
refactor: LisPy browser — engine fetches, virtual DOM queries, GUI renders

Architecture redesign per user direction:
- Headless engine is the BACKEND: fetch() → DOMParser → virtual DOM
- GUI iframe is the DISPLAY SURFACE: srcdoc injection, no network request
- (browser-read) queries virtual DOM — works headless, no iframe needed
- (browser-click/type) dispatches on rendered iframe — GUI interaction
- Engine and display are decoupled. Engine works without GUI.

New builtins: browser-links, browser-images, browser-meta, browser-status
Web Browser desktop icon added (🔎).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix ValuesView stub, deduplicate list_ops element equality

- Remove false __eq__ override from ValuesView in .pyi stub.
  The Rust implementation intentionally omits custom equality
  (matching Python's dict_values identity semantics), but the
  stub declared it, causing mypy to accept code relying on
  equality semantics that don't exist at runtime.

- Add type: ignore[comparison-overlap] to test assertions that
  intentionally verify ValuesView uses identity comparison.

- Extract element_eq() helper in list_ops.rs to deduplicate
  the Array/AoT match arms in item_count and item_index.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: disable heavy decorations on mobile for performance

- DotParticleCanvas: skip entire setup on touch devices (pointer:coarse)
- DotParticleCanvas: skip clearRect when no particles exist (idle optimization)
- GlitchCode: skip interval animation on touch devices
- ScrollDecodeCode: hidden on mobile (hidden sm:flex)
- Hide blur-[120px] blobs on mobile in Mission, Timeline, Awards sections

commit.message:
test: 27 Playwright tests for LisPy headless browser — all green

27 tests covering the full browser-within-vOS surface:
- Browser engine availability (2 tests)
- Loading pages, URL tracking, history (2 tests)
- Reading DOM: read, readAll, title, not-found (4 tests)
- DOM interaction: click, type (2 tests)
- JavaScript eval in page context (2 tests)
- Structured queries: query, queryAll, attribute (3 tests)
- HTML extraction (1 test)
- LisPy VM integration: browser-open, browser-title, browser-read,
  browser-click, browser-eval, browser-url, browser-read-all,
  browser-query (8 tests)
- Full workflow: open → read → click → type → verify (1 test)
- LisPy script driving browser end-to-end (1 test)
- Batch operations with browser actions (1 test)

Full suite: 77/77 passing (50 original + 27 new).

commit.message:
fix: Sprint 12 QA — Wizard API, confirm(), EntityTable actions, CLI await

Critical bug fixes found during QA review of Sprint 12:

Wizard API mismatch (pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Rewrote all wizard steps from { title, fields } to { label, render(data) }
- Wizard expects render functions returning HTML with data-field attrs
- Added initialData, validate functions, proper select rendering

confirm() signature (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Changed confirm('string') to confirm({ title, message, ... })
- confirm() destructures an object param, not a string

EntityTable actions (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Actions onClick receives single item from row menu, not array
- Wrapped single items in arrays: onClick: (item) => handler([item])

CLIService (alertCmd):
- Added async/await to state.update call in alert resolve

StateEngine seed data:
- Fixed proj-001 vpcs reference from 'Dev-VPC' to 'vpc-002' (UUID)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Track OpenSSF Scorecards for all repos (#126)

Signed-off-by: Jessica G <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Sprint 12 — LCM, PC Settings, VPCs, Alerts, Projects, Dashboard Gauges

- LCM (Life Cycle Manager): firmware/software inventory table, update scan
  simulation, rolling update workflow, update history tracking
- PC Settings: 7-tab config (General, NTP, DNS, SMTP, SSL, Pulse, SCMA)
  with save/persist for each section
- VPC Networking: VPCs with CIDR, subnets (overlay/VLAN), floating IP
  allocation, auto-generated route tables per VPC
- Alert Management: active/resolved alert list with severity cards,
  alert policy CRUD, SNMP/syslog configuration panel
- Projects: self-service portal with resource quotas (vCPU/memory/storage),
  user assignments, infrastructure scope, quota usage bars
- Dashboard SVG gauges: replaced linear bars with semi-circle SVG gauge
  charts for CPU, Memory, and Storage utilization
- Sidebar reorganized: added Networking section (VPCs, Flow), moved Alerts
  under Policies, added Projects/LCM/Settings under Admin
- CLI: 3 new commands (lcm_inventory, alert, project)
- Scenarios: 8 new guided exercises (33 total) covering LCM updates,
  PC settings, VPC networking, floating IPs, alerts, projects, SCMA
- Seed data: lcm_inventory (10), lcm_update_history (2), pc_settings (1),
  vpcs (2), subnets (4), floating_ips (2), alert_policies (2), projects (2)

commit.message:
feat: Sprint 11 — PC VMs, Calm/Marketplace, Audit Log, Multi-Cluster, Extended CLI & Scenarios

- PC VMs view: full implementation replacing placeholder, with categories,
  projects, resource summary cards, and cross-cluster column
- Calm/Marketplace: Blueprints CRUD, Application lifecycle, Marketplace
  with 8 pre-built items and clone-to-blueprints, Runbooks
- Audit Log: automatic event tracking on all state changes (create/update/
  delete), CSV export, filterable table
- Multi-Cluster Federation: register/unregister PE clusters, health checks,
  storage usage bars, node/VM counts
- CLI expanded: 12 new commands (calm, nuclei, upgrade_check,
  host_disks_check, links, df, uptime, whoami, hostname, date)
- Scenarios expanded: 10 new guided lab exercises (25 total) covering
  multi-cluster, Calm blueprints, marketplace, CLI mastery, NFS shares,
  FSVM verification, and audit trail review
- State seed data: registered_clusters, blueprints, applications, runbooks,
  audit_log collections with realistic seed entries
- Nav: sidebar updated with Clusters, Audit Log, Calm/Marketplace links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: particle canvas z-index architecture + STEM acronym + warmer copy

- Move DotParticleCanvas from z-0 (behind) to z-30 (above) with pointer-events-none
  so particles can freely cross section boundaries without being clipped
- Remove wrapper div around sections (no longer needed for z-index stacking)
- ResizeObserver now watches <main> (container) instead of wrapper div
- resizeCanvas reads container.scrollHeight for correct height with FAQ expansion
- Add STEM acronym explanation (S/T/E/M cards + description) in AboutSection
- Rewrite Hero, About, Mission, Features copy to be warmer and more approachable
  (less corporate/startup tone, more relatable club introduction)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): enforce trailing period rule and correct commit range

- Add subject-full-stop rule to commitlint so trailing periods are
  rejected by CI, matching what was already documented
- Fix commitlint job SHA range: use pull_request base/head SHAs on
  PR events and before/sha on push events so all commits are linted

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Separate per-client env config and rename "hosted" mode to "m365" (#8)

* Initial plan

* feat: separate env config per client, rename hosted to m365

- Split AppSettings into CoreSettings (core) + ApiSettings, ConsoleSettings, M365Settings (each package)
- Each client settings class reads from its own env file: .env.api, .env.console, .env.m365
- Created .env.api.example, .env.console.example, .env.m365.example; updated root .env.example to redirect
- Renamed AuthContext.hosted_user_token -> user_token; mode check now uses mode != "local"
- Renamed all hosted references to m365: create_hosted_app -> create_m365_app, _get_hosted_user_token -> _get_m365_user_token, _build_hosted_* -> _build_m365_*
- Changed auth_mode="hosted" to auth_mode="m365" in M365 app; auth_mode="api" in FastAPI app
- Renamed test_hosted_app.py -> test_m365_oauth_runtime.py with updated test names
- Updated README, docs/architecture.md, docs/m365-guide.md, docs/api-guide.md, docs/console-guide.md
- Updated .github/copilot-instructions.md and .github/instructions/ files
- All 107 tests pass, lint clean

commit.message:
Add KQL engine, query telemetry, DML commands, vector search, and Explorer enhancements

New features:
- KQL query engine with operators (where, project, extend, summarize, sort, top, take, distinct, count)
- Query telemetry store and activity tracking interfaces and storage implementations
- DML command service for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
- Vector embedding policy and vector index models
- KQL query controller and query telemetry controller in Host

Explorer (React):
- Container settings panel
- Import documents dialog
- KQL query editor component
- Query telemetry dashboard

Query engine improvements:
- Additional system functions and scalar subquery support
- Enhanced query explain service

Tests:
- KQL query executor tests
- DML command service, consistency middleware, indexing directive tests
- System function, scalar subquery, trigger execution, UDF query tests
- Vector search, unique key, query telemetry, TTL cleanup tests

feat: configurable storage backend with Explorer settings UI

Add support for selecting between SurrealDb, SQLite, and InMemory storage
backends via a side config file (emulator-config.json), CLI --storage
option, or the Explorer settings page.

Configuration:
- Add StorageType enum (SurrealDb, Sqlite, InMemory)
- Add Storage property to EmulatorOptions
- Load emulator-config.json from executable directory at startup
- Add --storage CLI option to the start command
- Priority: appsettings.json < emulator-config.json < CLI args

Storage backends:
- InMemoryDocumentStore: ConcurrentDictionary-based, ephemeral
- SqliteDocumentStore: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, single-file persistence
- SqliteChangeFeedProvider: persistent change feed in SQLite
- InMemory/SQLite activity and query telemetry stores
- DocumentStoreHelpers: shared logic for partition keys, patches, etc.
- StorageServiceRegistration: DI dispatch based on StorageType

Explorer settings UI:
- Storage Configuration card with backend dropdown and data directory
- GET/PUT /api/emulator/config endpoint for emulator-config.json
- Restart-required messaging after saving storage changes
- Storage type shown in the Configuration info card

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
#34950 Cleanup nano refetch commands in the background (#42472)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #34950

I changed from the original spec of 100 old commands to 3 due to load
test results. Admittedly my load test meant a very large number of hosts
all checked in and triggered deletion at once but at 100 per host and
per command the load was too high. 3 still results in cleanup over time
and doesn't seem to cause load issues.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [x] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,
`orbit/changes/` or `ee/fleetd-chrome/changes`.
See [Changes
files](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/guides/committing-changes.md#changes-files)
for more information.

- [x] Input data is properly validated, `SELECT *` is avoided, SQL
injection is prevented (using placeholders for values in statements), JS
inline code is prevented especially for url redirects, and untrusted
data interpolated into shell scripts/commands is validated against shell
metacharacters.
- [x] If paths of existing endpoints are modified without backwards
compatibility, checked the frontend/CLI for any necessary changes

## Testing

- [x] Added/updated automated tests
- [x] Where appropriate, [automated tests simulate multiple hosts and
test for host
isolation](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/reference/patterns-backend.md#unit-testing)
(updates to one hosts's records do not affect another)

- [x] QA'd all new/changed functionality manually

---------

commit.message:
Add YOLO-specific accuracy tuning guidance (#24)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spacemit-com/xslim/sessions/285e24a4-861e-42f8-8eb2-28efc36b07e8

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: alex-spacemit <213897846+alex-spacemit@users.noreply.github.com>

crt: C++ support (.init_array, libcxxrt), crt_initialized guard; piece.ld: .init_array section

tools/crt/crt0.c:
- Iterate .init_array on startup to call C++ static constructors
  and __attribute__((constructor)) functions (required for global
  objects with non-trivial constructors, e.g. vtable initialization).
- Add crt_initialized flag: pceAppProc00/pceAppExit00/pceAppNotify00
  all return early until pceAppInit() completes.  Guards against
  kernel notifications arriving before BSS is cleared and constructors
  have run, which caused alignment exceptions via uninitialized vtable
  pointers.
- crt_initialized is set AFTER pceAppInit() returns, so Proc/Exit/Notify
  are safe to use app state immediately.
- Switch BSS clear to word-by-word loop (both symbols are ALIGN(4)).
- Remove memset() header dependency from crt0.c; declare memset directly.

tools/crt/cxxrt.c (new):
- Minimal C++ runtime: __cxa_guard_acquire/release/abort (once-only
  init guards for local statics), __cxa_pure_virtual (abort stub),
  __cxa_atexit/__cxa_finalize (no-ops; no atexit support on P/ECE).

tools/crt/cxxnew.c (new):
- operator new/new[]/delete/delete[] backed by pceMalloc/pceFree.
- Placement new/delete inline stubs.

tools/crt/Makefile:
- Build cxxrt.o and cxxnew.o into libcxxrt.a in sysroot/lib/.
- Add libcxxrt.a to all/clean targets.

tools/piece.ld:
- Add .init_array section (ALIGN(4), KEEP, SORT) mapped to SRAM.
- Provides __init_array_start/__init_array_end symbols used by crt0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix test regressions and TUI wording/API UX

- remove incompatible LogisticRegression multi_class arg for current sklearn
- restore SyntheticECGGenerator compatibility surface (signal_length + generate_* methods)
- fix preprocessing regressions (baseline wander residual DC, exact denormalization, beat_length segmentation support)
- align segmentation dataset beat path with named args compatibility

- improve predict/explain error handling and model_id payload consistency

repository.description: A beginner-friendly, terminal-based ECG learning + ML analysis platform for education and research

commit.message:
fix: add D1 database_id to reuse existing orphaned database

The first failed deploy provisioned the D1 database but never
associated it with the worker. Unlike KV (which we deleted and
will auto-provision fresh), D1 needs the explicit database_id
to bind to the existing resource.

new_sqlite_classes migrations, not new_classes.

feat: wire admin app into worker entrypoint with D1 test support

feat: add admin UI with users and API keys management pages

- Users page: list, add, delete users; assign/change API keys; role badges
- API Keys page: list, add, delete keys with label and credential fields

feat: add D1 schema, AES-256-GCM encryption, and database access layer

- Create initial migration with api_keys and users tables
- api_keys stores encrypted client_id, api_key, and optional login_as
- users references api_keys with ON DELETE SET NULL foreign key
- AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt via Web Crypto API (64-char hex key)
- Full CRUD operations for both tables in src/db.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add OAuth infrastructure, styled pages, and MCP server relocation

- Move MCP server class to src/mcp/server.ts with Props generic type
- Add OAuth types, workers-oauth-utils, approval dialog, access handler
- Create dark teal themed styles, 404 page, and MCP landing page
- Add landing page and Airwallex function tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add session durable object with HMAC identity and token caching

- Derive deterministic session identity via HMAC-SHA256(secret, clientId:apiKey:loginAs)
- Create one Durable Object per credential tuple for token refresh serialization
- Cache Airwallex access tokens with 2-min refresh skew before expiry
- Set DO alarms for automatic token cleanup
- Retry once on 401 with forced re-auth
- Never store raw API key in DO storage, only fingerprints
- Extend Env type with D1, encryption, OAuth, and session state types

repository.description: Open standard for machine learning interoperability

commit.message:
fix: correct SAML login sessions column names

COLS.samlLoginSessions had wrong columns (relay_state, request_id, expires_at)
that don't exist in the saml_login_sessions table. Fixed to match actual schema:
id, tenant_id, provider_id, attorney_id, name_id, name_id_format, session_index,
created_at, updated_at.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use inline styles for Hero background to guarantee dev/prod consistency

All background colors and blur effects now use inline styles instead of
Tailwind classes. Inline styles are identical in dev and production builds,
eliminating any CSS compilation differences between localhost and Vercel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: darken all Hero glow orbs and dot pattern

- Animated orbs: indigo-600/15 → indigo-900/20, violet-600/10 → violet-950/20
- Parallax orbs: white/10 → white/5, indigo-400/20 → indigo-900/15, brand-200/10 → brand-900/10
- Dot pattern: rgba white 0.15 → 0.08, opacity-40 → opacity-30

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: darken Hero gradient - via-brand-600 was too bright

- Gradient: from-brand-900/via-brand-600/to-indigo-800 → from-gray-950/via-brand-900/to-indigo-950
- Glow orbs: reduced opacity (30%→15%, 20%→10%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add color-scheme dark and hardcoded dark bg/text fallback

Browser color-scheme defaults to light, causing native elements
to render in light mode on Vercel production. Raw CSS fallback
ensures dark background even before Tailwind classes apply.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
